name: Balcón de Siurana - Circular by cliffs and scree slopes
name_original: Balcón de Siurana - Circular por cingleras y riscales
region_intro: Jogging routes around Cornudella De Montsant traverse a diverse landscape, situated between the Montsant and Prades mountain ranges in Catalonia, Spain. The region features dramatic topography, including the rugged peaks and rocky walls of Serra del Montsant Natural Park, and the striking cliffs surrounding Siurana village and its reservoir. Runners can also explore picturesque river landscapes with natural pools, as well as extensive olive groves and vineyards. Best jogging routes around Cornudella De Montsant The most popular jogging route is Balcón de Siurana - Circular by cliffs and scree slopes, a 11.1 miles (17.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete. This challenging route offers views over the Siurana reservoir and surrounding cliffs. Another top favourite among local runners is Molí d'en Salvat loop from Siurana, a difficult 10.6 miles (17.1 km) path. This route features varied terrain, including paths through forests and along riverbeds. Local runners also love the Siurana Reservoir – Siurana Village and Cliffs loop from Cornudella de Montsant, a 8.8 miles (14.1 km) trail leading through the scenic Siurana area, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Jogging around Cornudella De Montsant is defined by dramatic mountain ranges, serene reservoirs, and extensive vineyards. The comprehensive network of trails offers options for various fitness levels, from moderate paths to more challenging mountain runs. The routes in Cornudella De Montsant are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Cornudella De Montsant's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of running trails can I expect around Cornudella De Montsant?
A: The region offers a diverse network of trails, ranging from easily accessible paths to challenging mountain runs with significant elevation gains. You'll find routes through rugged mountains, along serene reservoirs, and amidst extensive olive groves and vineyards. The terrain varies, providing options for different running preferences and fitness levels.

Q: How many jogging routes are available in the Cornudella De Montsant area?
A: There are over 20 running routes available around Cornudella De Montsant. These include a mix of moderate and difficult trails, ensuring options for various skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: While many routes in the area are rated moderate to difficult, the region does offer paths that are less strenuous. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31447254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornudella de Montsant – Siurana Reservoir loop from Cornudella de Montsant&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 12.2 km with a more manageable elevation gain.

Q: What are some challenging trail runs in Cornudella De Montsant?
A: For a more demanding experience, you can tackle routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951038490/balcon-de-siurana-circular-by-cliffs-and-scree-slopes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Siurana - Circular by cliffs and scree slopes&lt;/a&gt;, which is 17.8 km long with nearly 700 meters of elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31479129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Siurana Village and Cliffs – Roca Corbatera Summit loop from Cornudella de Montsant&lt;/a&gt;, a 24.3 km route with over 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular running trails in Cornudella De Montsant?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951038490/balcon-de-siurana-circular-by-cliffs-and-scree-slopes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Siurana - Circular by cliffs and scree slopes&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31461725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Siurana Reservoir – Siurana Village and Cliffs loop from Cornudella de Montsant&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landmarks or natural features can I see along the running routes?
A: The routes often pass through stunning landscapes. You can expect views of the Serra del Montsant Natural Park, the historic Siurana village perched on cliffs, and the scenic Siurana Reservoir. Some trails also lead past picturesque river landscapes with natural pools, such as the Cadolles Fondes Deep Pools (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/843527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cadolles Fondes Deep Pools&lt;/a&gt;), or offer glimpses of unique rock formations like The Throne of Siurana (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4614860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Throne of Siurana&lt;/a&gt;).

Q: Is Cornudella De Montsant a good place for trail running with dogs?
A: The region's extensive network of natural trails and open spaces makes it generally suitable for running with dogs. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially within the Serra del Montsant Natural Park, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. Always carry water for your dog, particularly on longer routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Cornudella De Montsant?
A: Spring and autumn are generally considered the best times for jogging in Cornudella De Montsant. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late evening runs preferable, while winters are cooler but still offer good conditions, especially on sunny days.

Q: Are there routes with significant elevation gain for training?
A: Absolutely. Cornudella De Montsant is situated between mountain ranges, offering routes with substantial elevation changes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31479129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Siurana Village and Cliffs – Roca Corbatera Summit loop from Cornudella de Montsant&lt;/a&gt; features over 1000 meters of ascent, making it ideal for challenging training runs.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Cornudella De Montsant?
A: The running routes in Cornudella De Montsant are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 15 reviews. Runners often praise the breathtaking views, the varied terrain, and the well-maintained paths that lead through stunning natural and cultural landscapes.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Cornudella De Montsant?
A: Yes, Cornudella de Montsant is a hub for outdoor activities, and many routes begin directly from the village. You can typically find parking in or near the village center, providing convenient access to various trailheads leading into the Prades Mountains, La Gritella, or Montsant.

Q: Can I reach the running trails using public transport?
A: While Cornudella de Montsant is accessible by public transport, options might be limited depending on your starting point. It's advisable to check local bus schedules in advance. Many trails start directly from the village, making it feasible to begin your run once you arrive by public transport.
